entities.name: HERBICIDES
entities.description: Chemical substances used to kill or inhibit the growth of unwanted plants

entities.name: WEED CONTROL
entities.description: The process of managing or eliminating weeds to improve crop yield

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Cultivated plants grown for food or other purposes

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area

entities.name: ECOLOGICAL THINKING
entities.description: An approach to understanding weeds based on their ecological roles and interactions

entities.name: WEEDS
entities.description: Unwanted plants that grow in agricultural or managed areas

entities.name: WEED BIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the biological processes and characteristics of weeds

entities.name: ECOLOGY
entities.description: The study of interactions between organisms and their environment

entities.name: ASIAN-PACIFIC REGION
entities.description: The geographical area encompassing Asia and the Pacific

entities.name: FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of generating food for human consumption

entities.name: ASIAN-PACIFIC WEED SCIENCE SOCIETY
entities.description: A professional organization promoting the exchange of ideas on weed control in the Asian-Pacific region

entities.name: COLONIZING SPECIES
entities.description: Species that opportunistically capture resources created by habitat disturbances

entities.name: HABITAT DISTURBANCES
entities.description: Changes in the environment that create opportunities for colonizing species

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE WEED CONTROL PRACTICES
entities.description: Weed management methods that are environmentally sound and viable long-term

entities.name: INTEGRATED WEED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A holistic approach to weed control combining biological, cultural, and chemical tools

entities.name: WEED SCIENCE
entities.description: The scientific study of weeds and their management

entities.name: CROP-WEED INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The biological and ecological relationships between crops and weeds

entities.name: COMPETITION
entities.description: The struggle between crops and weeds for limited resources

entities.name: ALLELOPATHY
entities.description: The chemical inhibition of one plant by another

entities.name: CRITICAL WEED-FREE PERIODS
entities.description: The specific growth stages of a crop where weed control is most essential for yield

entities.name: HERBICIDE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherited ability of a weed population to survive herbicide application

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns

entities.name: GENETICALLY-MODIFIED ORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms whose genetic material has been altered using genetic engineering

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The science or practice of farming

entities.name: WEEDY RICE
entities.description: A weed species closely related to cultivated rice

entities.name: BIO-RESOURCES
entities.description: Biological materials used for human benefit
